# ── Load required packages ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# fpp3 loads: tsibble (time series data), fable (models), feasts (features & plots),
#             ggplot2 (graphics), dplyr (data wrangling), lubridate (dates)
library(fpp3)
library(knitr)
install.packages("kableExtra")# kable() for publication-quality tables
library(kableExtra)  # enhanced table styling
library(scales)      # comma() for readable axis labels

# ── Read CSV, clean column names, parse dates, and create a tsibble ─────────────
# rename_with(trimws): removes trailing whitespace from raw CSV column headers
# my("Jan-80"): lubridate parses "Jan-80" format → Date object
# yearmonth():  converts Date → tsibble-compatible monthly index

wines_raw <- read.csv("AustralianWines.csv") |>
  rename_with(trimws) |>
  mutate(Month = yearmonth(my(Month))) |>
  as_tsibble(index = Month)

# Quick sanity check: 180 rows, 7 columns (Month + 6 wine types)
glimpse(wines_raw)



# ── Isolate Sparkling wine and rename Sales for cleaner code downstream ─────────
sparkling <- wines_raw |>
  select(Month, Sales = Sparkling)

# Confirm time span and key summary statistics
cat("Series:              Sparkling Wine\n")
cat("Time span:          ", format(min(sparkling$Month)), "to",
                            format(max(sparkling$Month)), "\n")
cat("Total observations: ", nrow(sparkling), "months\n")
cat("Overall mean:       ", round(mean(sparkling$Sales)), "thousand litres/month\n")
cat("December mean:      ", round(mean(sparkling$Sales[month(sparkling$Month)==12])),
    "thousand litres\n")
cat("June mean:          ", round(mean(sparkling$Sales[month(sparkling$Month)==6])),
    "thousand litres\n")
cat("Series max:         ", max(sparkling$Sales), "(Dec 1987)\n")
cat("Series min:         ", min(sparkling$Sales), "thousand litres\n")

# autoplot() on a tsibble produces a clean time series line chart
autoplot(sparkling, Sales) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title    = "Australian Sparkling Wine Sales (1980–1994)",
    subtitle = "Monthly retail sales — thousands of litres",
    y        = "Sales (thousands of litres)",
    x        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50"))

# gg_subseries() creates one mini-panel per calendar month
# Blue horizontal line = mean for that month across all years
sparkling |>
  gg_subseries(Sales) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title    = "Seasonal Subseries: Sparkling Wine (1980–1994)",
    subtitle = "Each panel = one calendar month | Blue line = month mean across all years",
    y        = "Sales (thousands of litres)",
    x        = "Year"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

# ACF(lag_max=36) checks for autocorrelation up to 3 years
# Seasonal series show spikes at multiples of the seasonal period (12, 24, 36)
sparkling |>
  ACF(Sales, lag_max = 36) |>
  autoplot() +
  labs(
    title    = "ACF: Sparkling Wine Sales (up to 36 months)",
    subtitle = "Blue dashed lines = 95% significance bounds"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)


sparkling |>
  model(STL(Sales ~ trend(window = 21) + season(window = "periodic"))) |>
  components() |>
  autoplot() +
  labs(title = "STL Decomposition: Sparkling Wine Sales (1980–1994)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)


## Interpretation

### Trend, Seasonality, and Noise

# Compute exact component magnitudes to support written interpretation
annual_means <- sparkling |>
  mutate(Year = year(Month)) |>
  group_by(Year) |>
  summarise(
    Ann_Mean  = mean(Sales),
    Dec_Sales = Sales[month(Month) == 12],
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

trend_change <- annual_means$Ann_Mean[annual_means$Year == 1993] -
                annual_means$Ann_Mean[annual_means$Year == 1980]

cat("Annual mean 1980:", round(annual_means$Ann_Mean[annual_means$Year==1980]),
    "thousand litres\n")
cat("Annual mean 1993:", round(annual_means$Ann_Mean[annual_means$Year==1993]),
    "thousand litres\n")
cat("Trend change:    ", round(trend_change), "thousand litres over 14 years\n")

# Compute December amplitude (absolute and ratio) for each training year
# This is the definitive test for additive vs. multiplicative seasonality


# Convert tsibble to plain dataframe to remove hidden index columns
sparkling_df <- as.data.frame(sparkling)

annual_avg <- sparkling_df |>
  mutate(Year = year(Month)) |>
  group_by(Year) |>
  summarise(Annual_Mean = round(mean(Sales)), .groups = "drop")

dec_sales <- sparkling_df |>
  mutate(Year = year(Month)) |>
  filter(month(Month) == 12) |>
  select(Year, Dec_Sales = Sales)

amp_tbl <- annual_avg |>
  left_join(dec_sales, by = "Year") |>
  mutate(
    Amplitude = Dec_Sales - Annual_Mean,
    Ratio     = round(Dec_Sales / Annual_Mean, 2)
  ) |>
  filter(Year < 1994)

colnames(amp_tbl)


# Methodology

## Partitioning Strategy


# ── Train/validation split: same 12-month holdout as Fortified wine example ─────
# Training:   Jan 1980 – Dec 1993  (168 months)
# Validation: Jan 1994 – Dec 1994  (12 months — held out entirely)

train <- sparkling |> filter(year(Month) < 1994)
valid <- sparkling |> filter(year(Month) >= 1994)

cat("Training set:   ", nrow(train), "observations (Jan 1980 – Dec 1993)\n")
cat("Validation set: ", nrow(valid), "observations (Jan 1994 – Dec 1994)\n")


## ETS Models Fitted

We fit **six models** covering the full spectrum from simple to complex, exactly
as specified in the assignment:

# ── Fit all models on the training set ────────────────────────────────────────
# All six models stored in a single mable — fit ONCE, reuse throughout.
# This avoids redundant refitting and ensures consistent parameter estimates.

fit <- train |>
  model(
    # ── Benchmark ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # Seasonal Naïve: forecast = same month in prior year (no smoothing at all)
    snaive    = SNAIVE(Sales),

   
  )



# ── View optimised smoothing parameters (MLE on training set) ──────────────────
# alpha: level smoothing — high alpha = trusts recent data more
# beta:  trend smoothing — high beta = trend adapts quickly
# gamma: seasonal smoothing — high gamma = seasonal indices update quickly
# phi:   damping factor (hw_damped only) — < 1 flattens trend over horizon
# l[0], b[0], s[0..11]: initial state values optimised jointly with alpha/beta/gamma

fit |>
  tidy() |>
  filter(.model != "snaive") |>
  select(.model, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
  kable(
    digits    = 4,
    caption   = "Table 2: Optimised ETS Smoothing Parameters (MLE on 1980–1993 Training Data)"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE)

# ── In-sample information criteria (AIC, AICc, BIC) ────────────────────────────
# AICc (corrected AIC) balances fit vs. model complexity on the TRAINING set.
# Lower AICc = preferred model in-sample.
# Note: AICc measures in-sample fit; MASE measures out-of-sample accuracy.
# Both are reported for completeness.

fit |>
  glance() |>
  filter(.model != "snaive") |>
  select(.model, AIC, AICc, BIC) |>
  arrange(AICc) |>
  kable(
    digits    = 1,
    col.names = c("Model", "AIC", "AICc", "BIC"),
    caption   = "Table 3: In-Sample Information Criteria — Sorted by AICc (lower = better)"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE)


# Results

## Forecast Accuracy

### Generate 12-Month Forecasts


# ── Generate 12-month-ahead forecasts for all six models ──────────────────────
# forecast() reuses the already-fitted `fit` mable — no refitting required.
# h = 12: forecast the 12 months of 1994 (the validation period)
fc <- fit |> forecast(h = 12)



# Show last 3 training years + full 1994 validation year for context
sparkling_recent <- sparkling |> filter(year(Month) >= 1991)

fc_tbl <- fc |>
  as_tibble() |>
  select(.model, Month, .mean)

fc_tbl <- fc |>
  as_tibble() |>
  select(.model, Month, .mean)

ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = as.data.frame(sparkling) |> 
              filter(year(Month) >= 1991),
            aes(x = as.Date(Month), y = Sales),
            colour = "black", linewidth = 1.0) +
  geom_line(data = fc_tbl |>
              mutate(Month = as.Date(Month)),
            aes(x = Month, y = .mean, colour = .model, linetype = .model),
            linewidth = 0.85) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "snaive"    = "#95A5A6",
    "ses"       = "#E74C3C",
    "holt"      = "#E67E22",
    "hw_add"    = "#3498DB",
    "hw_mult"   = "#27AE60",
    "hw_damped" = "#8E44AD"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title    = "Sparkling Wine: All ETS Forecasts vs. Actual 1994 Sales",
    subtitle = "Black = observed | Coloured lines = model forecasts",
    y        = "Sales (thousands of litres)",
    x        = NULL,
    colour   = "Model",
    linetype = "Model"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")



# ── Compute all five accuracy metrics on the hold-out validation set ────────────
# accuracy(fc, sparkling) matches each forecast to the observed 1994 values.
# ME:   Mean Error — positive = over-forecast; negative = under-forecast
# RMSE: Root Mean Squared Error — penalises large errors more
# MAE:  Mean Absolute Error — average absolute miss per month
# MAPE: Mean Absolute Percentage Error — scale-free % miss
# MASE: Mean Absolute Scaled Error — ratio to SNAIVE MAE (< 1 beats naive)

acc <- accuracy(fc, sparkling) |>
  select(.model, ME, RMSE, MAE, MAPE, MASE) |>
  arrange(MASE)

acc |>
  kable(
    digits    = 3,
    col.names = c("Model", "ME", "RMSE", "MAE", "MAPE (%)", "MASE"),
    caption   = "Table 4: Out-of-Sample Forecast Accuracy — 1994 Validation Set (sorted by MASE)"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#d4efdf",
           extra_css = "border: 2px solid #27AE60;") |>
  row_spec(which(acc$.model %in% c("ses", "holt")),
           background = "#fde8e8") |>
  footnote(
    general       = "Green highlight = best model. Red highlight = models that ignore seasonality.",
    general_title = "Note:"
  )


# Extract key values for inline narrative
best_name   <- acc$.model[1]
best_mase   <- acc$MASE[1]
best_mae    <- acc$MAE[1]
snaive_mase <- acc$MASE[acc$.model == "snaive"]
ses_mase    <- acc$MASE[acc$.model == "ses"]
holt_mase   <- acc$MASE[acc$.model == "holt"]
pct_better  <- (1 - best_mase / snaive_mase) * 100
above1      <- acc |> filter(MASE > 1)

cat("Best model:              ", best_name, "\n")
cat("Best MASE:               ", round(best_mase, 3), "\n")
cat("Improvement over SNAIVE: ", round(pct_better, 1), "%\n")
cat("Best MAE:                ", round(best_mae, 1), "thousand litres/month\n\n")
cat("Models with MASE > 1 (worse than naive):\n")
if (nrow(above1) > 0) {
  print(above1 |> select(.model, MASE))
} else {
  cat("  None\n")
}

# Extract best model from the already-fitted mable — dynamically determined
cat("Diagnosing residuals for best model:", best_name, "\n")
best_fit <- fit |> select(all_of(best_name))


### Three-Panel Diagnostic Plot


# gg_tsresiduals() produces three panels in one call:
# Panel 1: innovation residuals over time (should look random, centred at zero)
# Panel 2: ACF of residuals (all bars should lie within 95% confidence bands)
# Panel 3: histogram of residuals (should be bell-shaped, centred at zero)

best_fit |>
  gg_tsresiduals(lag_max = 36) +
  labs(
    title    = paste("Residual Diagnostics:", best_name, "(Sparkling Wine, 1980–1993)"),
    subtitle = "Top: residuals over time | Middle: ACF of residuals | Bottom: histogram"
  )


### Ljung-Box White Noise Test

# ── Ljung-Box test at lag = 24 (2 years × 12 months/year) ─────────────────────
# H0: the first 24 residual autocorrelations are all zero (white noise)
# p > 0.05: fail to reject H0 → model adequate → DEPLOY
# p <= 0.05: reject H0 → structure remains → DO NOT DEPLOY yet

lb_result <- best_fit |>
  augment() |>
  features(.innov, ljung_box, lag = 24)

lb_result |>
  select(.model, lb_stat, lb_pvalue) |>
  kable(
    digits    = 4,
    col.names = c("Model", "Ljung-Box Q (lag=24)", "p-value"),
    caption   = "Table 5: Ljung-Box Test — Best Model (lag = 24)"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE)


# Comparing residual ACF patterns across models reveals which leave structure behind
# SNAIVE will show lag-12 spikes (it never updates seasonal estimates)
# HW models should have clean ACFs if seasonality is well-captured

fit |>
  augment() |>
  ACF(.innov, lag_max = 36) |>
  autoplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ .model, ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title    = "Residual ACF by Model — Sparkling Wine",
    subtitle = "Blue bands = 95% bounds | Clean ACF = model adequately captures series structure"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

# Compare white noise test results across all six models
fit |>
  augment() |>
  features(.innov, ljung_box, lag = 24) |>
  select(.model, lb_stat, lb_pvalue) |>
  arrange(desc(lb_pvalue)) |>
  kable(
    digits    = 4,
    col.names = c("Model", "Q Statistic", "p-value"),
    caption   = "Table 6: Ljung-Box Test (lag=24) Across All Models — Higher p = cleaner residuals"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE)

# Compute exact residual statistics for evidence-based interpretation
resid_data <- best_fit |> augment() |> select(Month, .innov) |> na.omit()

resid_mean <- mean(resid_data$.innov)
resid_sd   <- sd(resid_data$.innov)
skew_val   <- mean(((resid_data$.innov - resid_mean)/resid_sd)^3)
kurt_val   <- mean(((resid_data$.innov - resid_mean)/resid_sd)^4)
n_train    <- nrow(resid_data)
ci_resid   <- 1.96 / sqrt(n_train)

# Residual ACF at key lags
resid_acf  <- best_fit |> augment() |> ACF(.innov, lag_max=36) |> as_tibble()
r_lag1     <- resid_acf$acf[resid_acf$lag == 1]
r_lag12    <- resid_acf$acf[resid_acf$lag == 12]
sig_lags   <- resid_acf |> filter(lag > 0, abs(acf) > ci_resid) |> pull(lag)
p_val      <- lb_result$lb_pvalue

# Variance stability
n_half     <- n_train %/% 2
var_ratio  <- var(resid_data$.innov[(n_half+1):n_train]) /
              var(resid_data$.innov[1:n_half])

# December residuals specifically
dec_resid_mean <- mean(resid_data$.innov[month(resid_data$Month) == 12])

within_2sd <- mean(abs(resid_data$.innov - resid_mean) <= 2*resid_sd) * 100

cat("--- Residual Summary ---\n")
cat("Mean:            ", round(resid_mean, 1), "\n")
cat("Std deviation:   ", round(resid_sd, 1), "\n")
cat("Skewness:        ", round(skew_val, 3), "\n")
cat("Kurtosis:        ", round(kurt_val, 3), "\n")
cat("Within 2 SD:     ", round(within_2sd, 1), "%  (normal = 95%)\n")
cat("Variance ratio:  ", round(var_ratio, 3), "(2nd half / 1st half)\n")
cat("Dec resid mean:  ", round(dec_resid_mean, 1), "\n")
cat("ACF lag 1:       ", round(r_lag1, 4),
    ifelse(abs(r_lag1) > ci_resid, " SIGNIFICANT", " within bounds"), "\n")
cat("ACF lag 12:      ", round(r_lag12, 4),
    ifelse(abs(r_lag12) > ci_resid, " SIGNIFICANT", " within bounds"), "\n")
cat("Significant lags:", ifelse(length(sig_lags)==0, "none", paste(sig_lags, collapse=", ")), "\n")
cat("Ljung-Box p:     ", round(p_val, 4), "\n")

# Theoretical prediction intervals assume normally distributed errors
# 80% PI: plausible range for 8 out of 10 future months
# 95% PI: wider range capturing more extreme scenarios

fc_best <- best_fit |> forecast(h = 12)

fc_best |>
  autoplot(sparkling |> filter(year(Month) >= 1991), level = c(80, 95)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title    = paste("Forecast with Prediction Intervals:", best_name),
    subtitle = "Dark band: 80% PI | Light band: 95% PI | Black line: observed sales",
    y = "Sales (thousands of litres)", x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)


# ── Empirical prediction interval from actual hold-out errors ──────────────────
# These are computed from the 12 actual 1994 validation errors
val_errors <- valid$Sales - fc_best$.mean

cat("Empirical Prediction Interval (12 hold-out validation errors):\n")
cat("  5th  percentile:", round(quantile(val_errors, 0.05)), "thousand litres\n")
cat(" 95th  percentile:", round(quantile(val_errors, 0.95)), "thousand litres\n")
cat("\n Month-by-month 1994 forecast accuracy:\n")

data.frame(
  Month    = format(valid$Month),
  Actual   = valid$Sales,
  Forecast = round(fc_best$.mean),
  Error    = round(val_errors),
  Pct_Err  = round(abs(val_errors)/valid$Sales * 100, 1)
) |>
  kable(
    col.names = c("Month", "Actual", "Forecast", "Error", "Abs % Error"),
    caption   = "Table 7: Month-by-Month 1994 Validation Results"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE)



# This section previews Chapter 7 by demonstrating how differencing removes
# the non-stationary components (trend and seasonality) identified in the EDA.
# A stationary series (bottom panel) is the prerequisite for ARIMA modelling.

sparkling |>
  mutate(
    diff1    = difference(Sales, 1),      # first difference (lag 1): removes trend
    diff12   = difference(Sales, 12),     # seasonal diff (lag 12): removes seasonality
    diff1_12 = difference(diff1, 12)      # both: achieves approximate stationarity
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(Sales, diff1, diff12, diff1_12),
    names_to  = "Series",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) |>
  mutate(Series = factor(Series,
    levels = c("Sales", "diff1", "diff12", "diff1_12"),
    labels = c("Original (non-stationary)",
               "1st Difference — trend removed (d=1)",
               "Seasonal Difference lag-12 — seasonality removed (D=1)",
               "Both Differences — approximately stationary (d=1, D=1)"))) |>
  autoplot(Value) +
  facet_wrap(~ Series, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  labs(title    = "Differencing Preview: Bridge to ARIMA (Chapter 7)",
       subtitle = "Applying d=1 and D=1 at lag 12 produces a stationary series",
       y = "Value", x = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# ── Deployment checklist — mirrors Section 9 of the Fortified wine starter script
mase_val  <- acc$MASE[acc$.model == best_name]
me_val    <- acc$ME[acc$.model == best_name]
p_val_dep <- lb_result$lb_pvalue

cat("=================================================================\n")
cat(" DEPLOYMENT CHECKLIST — SPARKLING WINE (", best_name, ")\n")
cat(" [Same three gates as Fortified wine Section 9]\n")
cat("=================================================================\n\n")

gate1 <- mase_val < 1
gate2 <- p_val_dep > 0.05
gate3 <- abs(me_val) < 250

cat("GATE 1 — MASE < 1.0 (beats seasonal naive benchmark):\n")
cat(" MASE =", round(mase_val, 3),
    "->", ifelse(gate1,
    paste0("PASS — beats naive by ", round((1-mase_val)*100,1), "%"),
    "FAIL — does not beat naive"), "\n\n")

cat("GATE 2 — Ljung-Box p > 0.05 (residuals are white noise):\n")
cat(" p =", round(p_val_dep, 4),
    "->", ifelse(gate2,
    "PASS — residuals consistent with white noise",
    "CONCERN — residual autocorrelation detected"), "\n\n")

cat("GATE 3 — ME operationally acceptable (low systematic bias):\n")
cat(" ME =", round(me_val, 1), "thousand litres ->",
    ifelse(gate3,
    paste0("PASS — bias of ",
           round(abs(me_val)/mean(sparkling$Sales)*100,1), "% of series mean"),
    "CONCERN — notable systematic bias"), "\n\n")

cat("-----------------------------------------------------------------\n")
if (gate1 & gate2) {
  cat("FINAL VERDICT: DEPLOY\n")
  cat("All gates satisfied. Model is adequate for production forecasting.\n")
} else if (gate1 & !gate2) {
  cat("FINAL VERDICT: DEPLOY WITH CAUTION\n")
  cat("Beats benchmark but residual structure remains.\n")
} else {
  cat("FINAL VERDICT: DO NOT DEPLOY\n")
  cat("Model fails to beat seasonal naive.\n")
}
cat("=================================================================\n")

# Visual summary of the deployment decision
data.frame(
  Gate      = c("Gate 1 — Accuracy",
                "Gate 2 — Residual Quality",
                "Gate 3 — Bias"),
  Metric    = c(paste0("MASE = ", round(mase_val, 3)),
                paste0("LB p = ",  round(p_val_dep, 4)),
                paste0("ME = ",    round(me_val, 1), " K litres")),
  Threshold = c("< 1.0  (beats SNAIVE)",
                "> 0.05 (white noise)",
                "< 250K litres (< 10% of mean)"),
  Result    = c(ifelse(gate1, "PASS", "FAIL"),
                ifelse(gate2, "PASS", "CONCERN"),
                ifelse(gate3, "PASS", "CONCERN"))
) |>
  kable(caption = "Table 8: Deployment Decision Gate Summary") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  column_spec(4, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(c(gate1, gate2, gate3), "#27AE60", "#E67E22"))

acc |>
  mutate(
    Beats_Naive = ifelse(MASE < 1, "Yes", "No"),
    Rank        = row_number()
  ) |>
  select(Rank, .model, MASE, RMSE, ME, Beats_Naive) |>
  kable(
    digits    = 3,
    col.names = c("Rank", "Model", "MASE", "RMSE", "ME", "Beats SNAIVE?"),
    caption   = "Table 9: Final Model Ranking — 1994 Validation Set"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#d4efdf") |>
  row_spec(which(acc$.model %in% c("ses","holt")), background = "#fde8e8")

# Code to explore:
fit_sarima <- train |> model(sarima = ARIMA(Sales))
fit_sarima |> report()   # shows selected SARIMA order

# Code to explore:
fit_xreg <- train |>
  mutate(xmas = as.numeric(month(Month) == 12)) |>
  model(hw_xreg = ETS(Sales ~ xmas + error("A") + trend("A") + season("M")))
